regions.name: Awadh
regions.intro_markdown: Awadhi cuisine, born in the royal kitchens of Lucknow, is famous for its dum (slow-cooking) technique, its delicate kebabs, and its layered biryanis. The flavours are subtle, aromatic, and unhurried.

regions.name: Tamil Nadu
regions.intro_markdown: Tamil cooking favours rice, lentils, tamarind, coconut, and curry leaves. Vegetarian traditions run deep, and breakfasts of dosa, idli, and uttapam are eaten across the south.

regions.name: Punjab
regions.intro_markdown: Punjabi food is generous and richly flavoured: tandoori meats, dal makhani, robust breads, and a love of butter and cream.
